source: Collaborative Multi-Level Embedding Learning from Reviews for Rating Prediction

concepts.finding: Multi-level embedding from review text: word-level (topic modeling), sentence-level (sentiment analysis), document-level (review summary). Multi-task learning jointly trains all levels with rating prediction. Attention mechanism weighs different review levels for prediction. Outperforms single-level review models.
concepts.implication: Multi-level keyword representation: character-level (subword patterns, language), word-level (semantics), phrase-level (compound keywords), document-level (pages where keyword appears). Each level captures different intent signals.
concepts.open_question: What granularity of keyword representation (char, word, phrase, document) is most informative for intent clustering?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Multi-level keyword embeddings (character + word + document-level) produce more robust intent clusters than single-level embeddings, especially for short (1-2 word) keywords.
